chief_complaint: A 23-year-old male patient presented with unconsciousness and fever after consuming excessive amounts of alcohol. He was found to have hyperthermia, tachycardia, seizures, coma, and hypoglycemia.
history: The patient had no previous history of abnormal thyroid function. He was admitted to the hospital's emergency department after being found unconscious on a roadside.
exam: Hyperthermia (40.8 degrees C), nodal tachycardia (180 beats/min), seizures, coma
labs: Severe thyroid dysfunction (T3 = 6.67 nmol/L, T4 = 252.00 nmol/L, free T3 = 29.20 pmol/L, free T4 = 65.30 pmol/L, and TSH = 0.001 muIU/mL), hypoglycemia (2.18 mmol/L), multiple computed tomography scans revealed brain abnormalities, magnetic resonance imaging revealed bilateral frontal lobe lesions
